title: 2x4 Truss Farm Bench
summary: A compact 2x4 dining bench with an X-cross truss below the seat — matches this catalog's 2x4 truss table, distinct from the simpler beginner farm bench.
description:
This bench pairs with this catalog's 2x4 truss table: same angled-2x4 leg-and-truss language, scaled down to bench height and length. Build the two ends first — they're identical — using either screws directly or a pocket-hole jig set to the 1-1/2" setting.

The bottom stretcher is straightforward, screwed on with 2-1/2" screws. The top stretcher is the fussier of the two: a pocket-hole jig is the clean way to attach it, but the plan gives a genuine no-jig alternative — toe-nailing screws in at an angle — and notes that the top stretcher gets reinforced by the seat boards anyway, so a slightly rougher top joint here isn't a structural risk.

The cross supports (the visual "truss" this bench is named for) attach through the seat boards from above with 2-1/2" screws, and at the bottom either with pocket holes or the same toe-nail method used on the top stretcher — screwing straight down about 1-1/2" from the angled tip. Leave a small 1/8" gap between the seat boards if the bench will live outdoors, so water has somewhere to go instead of pooling on the seat.

tools.note: 10° and 45° bevel cuts on the legs and cross supports.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Both ends beveled at 10° off square, ends parallel.
Long point to long point, both ends cut at 10° off square, not parallel.
Both ends cut at 45° off square, ends parallel.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut two leg tops at 14", four legs at 15-1/4", two leg cross braces at 12-1/2", one bottom stretcher at 34", one top stretcher at 38", two cross supports at 13-3/8" and four seat boards at 54" from the 2x4. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to five 8 ft 2x4. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.

steps.title: Build the two ends
steps.body: Build two identical end assemblies from the leg tops, legs, and cross braces, using screws or a pocket-hole jig set to 1-1/2".

steps.title: Add the stretchers
steps.body: Attach the bottom stretcher with 2-1/2" screws. Attach the top stretcher with a pocket-hole jig if available, or by toe-nailing screws in at an angle — the top stretcher gets reinforced by the seat boards in a later step regardless.

steps.title: Add the cross supports
steps.body: Attach the cross supports through the top with 2-1/2" screws. For the bottom, use pocket holes or the toe-nail method — screwing straight down about 1-1/2" from the angled tip end.

steps.title: Add the seat boards and finish
steps.body: Attach the seat boards from the underside with 2-1/2" screws, leaving a small 1/8" gap between boards if the bench will be used outdoors, for water drainage. Sand and finish.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: 2x4 farm bench with an X-cross truss below the seat
